title: System Physics Schema V1
description: JSON Schema for Project Lumina System Physics documents. Defines the structured SOP for the top-level Conversational Interface (CI) layer — the invariants, standing orders, escalation triggers, and output contract that govern the CI regardless of which domain is active. Derived from YAML source (cfg/system-physics.yaml); the compiled JSON form's SHA-256 hash is committed to the system CTL before operational activation.

id.description: Globally unique system physics identifier. Format: system/{org}/{name}/v{major}

meta_authority_id.description: ID of the Meta Authority responsible for this system physics document. Use 'root' for the top-level governance body.

conforms_to.description: Version of lumina-core this system-physics document targets (e.g. 'lumina-core-v1.0.0')

description.description: Human-readable description of this system physics document's scope and purpose

invariants.description: List of system-layer invariants. Must include at least one critical invariant. These apply unconditionally — before and regardless of which domain is active.

standing_orders.description: List of bounded automated CI responses the orchestrator may take when a system invariant is violated

escalation_triggers.description: Conditions that cause the system layer to escalate to the Meta Authority

ci_output_contract.description: Structured rules governing CI output behaviour. These are the machine-readable source of truth for the human-readable rendering in specs/global-system-prompt-v1.md.

output_mode.description: Primary output mode. 'conversational_text' means user-facing prose only; no structured data payloads unless explicitly overridden.

chain_of_thought.description: Whether the CI may surface internal reasoning steps to the user.

raw_json_output.description: When raw JSON may appear in CI output.

grounding_requirement.description: How tightly CI responses must be grounded in the active prompt contract.

capability_claims.description: Whether capability claims are bounded by the active prompt contract (prevents false advertising of hidden features).

internal_state_disclosure.description: Whether internal confidence scores, private policy internals, or sensitive runtime state may be disclosed.

glossary.description: Controlled vocabulary for Lumina infrastructure terms. Entries are surfaced via the glossary-intercept pipeline when admins or auditors query term definitions in the system domain.

id.description: Unique identifier for this invariant within the system physics document

severity.description: critical: violation halts autonomous CI action and triggers immediate standing order or escalation; warning: triggers a standing order

check.description: Predicate expression evaluated by the orchestrator. Required when handled_by is absent.

handled_by.description: ID of the subsystem that evaluates this invariant. When present, the orchestrator defers to the named subsystem.

standing_order_on_violation.description: ID of the standing order to invoke when this invariant is violated

signal_type.description: Optional signal type emitted when this invariant fires

id.description: Unique identifier for this standing order

action.description: The automated action the CI takes when this standing order is activated

trigger_condition.description: Invariant ID or condition that activates this order

max_attempts.description: Maximum times this order may be applied per session before escalation

escalation_on_exhaust.description: Whether to escalate to the Meta Authority when max_attempts is reached

description.description: Human-readable description of what this standing order does

condition.description: The condition that triggers escalation to the Meta Authority

target_role.description: Role of the Meta Authority receiving this escalation

sla_minutes.description: SLA for acknowledgement in minutes; breach is itself a reportable event in the system CTL

term.description: Canonical term (lowercase). The primary lookup key.

definition.description: Concise, administrator-appropriate definition of the infrastructure term.

aliases.description: Alternate forms an administrator or auditor might use.

related_terms.description: Other glossary terms to surface alongside this definition.

example_in_context.description: A concrete example using the term in a Lumina operational context.
